# UC-17.3.27 · Cato Threat Prevention Events (Cato Networks)

## Description

Cato IPS blends machine learning and signatures inline on all traversing traffic. Because enforcement runs at Cato PoPs, IPS coverage is uniform for every site and remote user without shipping appliances to each location. Tracking blocked threats, source context, targeted services, and attack patterns supports incident triage, threat hunting, and validation that prevention is active everywhere.

## Value

Cato IPS blends machine learning and signatures inline on all traversing traffic. Because enforcement runs at Cato PoPs, IPS coverage is uniform for every site and remote user without shipping appliances to each location. Tracking blocked threats, source context, targeted services, and attack patterns supports incident triage, threat hunting, and validation that prevention is active everywhere.

## Implementation

Enrich `src_ip` with asset and identity lookups. Create notables for rare threats, cross-site recurrence of the same source, or blocks against critical server subnets. Tune out known vulnerability scanners only with documented exceptions. Correlate spikes with Cato configuration changes (UC-17.3.28).

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Cato Networks Events App (Splunkbase 8037); `eventsFeed.py` from [catonetworks/cato-splunk-integration](https://github.com/catonetworks/cato-splunk-integration).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Cato threat / IPS events; `sourcetype=cato:events` or `cato:sase`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Enrich `src_ip` with asset and identity lookups. Create notables for rare threats, cross-site recurrence of the same source, or blocks against critical server subnets. Tune out known vulnerability scanners only with documented exceptions. Correlate spikes with Cato configuration changes (UC-17.3.28).

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=sase sourcetype IN ("cato:events","cato:sase") earliest=-24h
| eval blocked=if(match(lower(coalesce(action, disposition, verdict, "")),"(?i)block|prevent|deny|drop"),1,0)
| eval threat=coalesce(threat_name, signature_name, rule_name, description, "unknown")
| eval dst_service=coalesce(dst_port, service_name, app_name)
| where blocked=1 AND match(lower(coalesce(event_sub_type, category, "")),"(?i)ips|intrusion|threat|exploit")
| stats count values(threat) as threats values(dst_ip) as dst_ips by src_ip, site_name, dst_service
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Cato Threat Prevention Events (Cato Networks)** — Cato IPS blends machine learning and signatures inline on all traversing traffic. Because enforcement runs at Cato PoPs, IPS coverage is uniform for every site and remote user without shipping appliances to each location. Tracking blocked threats, source context, targeted services, and attack patterns supports incident triage, threat hunting, and validation that prevention is active everywhere.

Documented **Data sources**: Cato threat / IPS events; `sourcetype=cato:events` or `cato:sase`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Cato Networks Events App (Splunkbase 8037); `eventsFeed.py` from [catonetworks/cato-splunk-integration](https://github.com/catonetworks/cato-splunk-integration). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: sase.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=sase, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **blocked** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **threat** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **dst_service** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where blocked=1 AND match(lower(coalesce(event_sub_type, category, "")),"(?i)ips|intrusion|threat|exploit")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by src_ip, site_name, dst_service** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the Cato Management Application for the same sites, rules, and time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (top blocked flows), Bar chart (threats by site), Map (src_geo if present), Timeline (block burst detection).

## SPL

```spl
index=sase sourcetype IN ("cato:events","cato:sase") earliest=-24h
| eval blocked=if(match(lower(coalesce(action, disposition, verdict, "")),"(?i)block|prevent|deny|drop"),1,0)
| eval threat=coalesce(threat_name, signature_name, rule_name, description, "unknown")
| eval dst_service=coalesce(dst_port, service_name, app_name)
| where blocked=1 AND match(lower(coalesce(event_sub_type, category, "")),"(?i)ips|intrusion|threat|exploit")
| stats count values(threat) as threats values(dst_ip) as dst_ips by src_ip, site_name, dst_service
| sort -count
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic
  by All_Traffic.user, All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest span=1h
| sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table (top blocked flows), Bar chart (threats by site), Map (src_geo if present), Timeline (block burst detection).
